input_dna_matches.expected_link: Mennonite colony regional/endogamy signal; not proof of Anna Friesen as direct ancestor
small Mennonite founder-loop signal; not a direct relationship anchor

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Gregory Batensky, son of Ukrainian Orthodox parents: listed father Batensky and mother from local Orthodox community. No Mennonite mother recorded.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: blocks Anna Friesen as mother of Gregory Batensky.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Birth of Anna Friesen, daughter of Jakob Wiens household connection through mother Maria Jantzen; colony register only.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: confirms a Mennonite Anna Friesen exists in Molotschna but not linked to Batensky family.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Another Anna Friesen born in Chortitza colony to different parents.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: separates I9 from I5; same-name colony duplication.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Wiens, Jantzen, Friesen and Schmidt households listed repeatedly across colony villages.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: supports founder-loop/endogamy context; not direct parentage.

input_archive_snippets.transcription_excerpt: Ludmila Friesen shown as mother/ancestor bridge from Batensky to Molotschna Mennonites; no document cited.
Birth of Alexander Batensky, son of Gregory Batensky and Matrona Dodatko.
input_archive_snippets.expected_use: anchors Batensky/Dodatko branch and blocks direct Mennonite parent insertion.

input_user_assertions.assertion: Ludmila Friesen is the missing bridge between Batensky and Mennonite DNA matches.
input_user_assertions.evidence: created as placeholder to explain DNA

input_user_assertions.assertion: Anna Friesen from Molotschna is mother of Gregory Batensky.
input_user_assertions.evidence: Mennonite DNA matches and same region

input_user_assertions.assertion: All Anna Friesen entries in South Russia are same person.

embedded_errors.reason: placeholder person with no source created to explain DNA
Mennonite regional DNA/locality signal used as direct Batensky parentage
same name/year but different colony and parent set
18 cM across small segments in founder population
public GEDCOM bridge lacks primary source and conflicts with Orthodox records

expected_reasoning_chain: Step 1: Parse GEDCOM and identify I11 Ludmila Friesen as a bridge person with no source, no parents, no vital records, and only bridge purpose.
Step 2: Use src_1301 and src_1306 to confirm Batensky/Dodatko Orthodox/Slavic parentage for Gregory and Alexander.
Step 3: Use src_1302 to confirm Anna Friesen exists in Molotschna but not in Batensky household or Orthodox records.
Step 4: Use src_1303 to separate Chortitza Anna Friesen from Molotschna Anna Friesen.
Step 5: Use src_1304 to mark Mennonite surnames as repeated founder-loop surnames, requiring multi-path and colony-register caution.
Step 6: Evaluate dna_1301/dna_1302/dna_1303 as Mennonite colony cluster, not a direct ancestor proof.
Step 7: Evaluate dna_1304/dna_1305 as Batensky/Dodatko branch anchors and give them higher branch-placement priority.
Step 8: Downweight dna_1306 small segments under Mennonite founder-loop correction.
Step 9: Reject Ludmila Friesen bridge and Anna Friesen-as-mother claim.
Step 10: Preserve Mennonite cluster as a separate probable regional DNA cluster requiring future triangulation/records, not as direct pedigree insertion.

relationship_Anna_Friesen_mother_of_Greg.reason: primary Orthodox records conflict; no marriage/adoption/conversion evidence

identity_I5_I9_same_person.reason: same name but different colony and parent set

mennonite_colony_cluster.reason: Friesen/Wiens/Jantzen shared matches and colony records, but founder-loop corrected

direct_mennonite_ancestor_for_proband.reason: regional DNA cluster exists but no direct pedigree bridge

true_lineage: I3 Gregory Batensky + I4 Matrona Dodatko
Batensky/Dodatko branch is Slavic/Orthodox in this synthetic tree
true_cluster_status: Mennonite Friesen/Wiens/Jantzen/Schmidt cluster exists as DNA/regional signal.
No confirmed direct ancestor bridge from Batensky to Friesen in this synthetic tree.
Ludmila Friesen is fictional placeholder and must be rejected.
false_claims: Ludmila Friesen is a real bridge ancestor.
Anna Friesen is mother of Gregory Batensky.
All Anna Friesen records from South Russia are the same person.
Mennonite DNA cluster automatically proves a direct Mennonite ancestor.

evaluation_assertions.description: Engine must reject Ludmila Friesen fictional bridge.
Engine must reject Anna Friesen as mother of Gregory Batensky.
Engine must not auto-merge Molotschna Anna Friesen and Chortitza Anna Friesen.
Engine must classify Mennonite matches as a separate probable cluster, not confirmed direct ancestry.
